name: Funding Pips
description_short: Dubai-based prop firm offering 2-step, 1-step, and instant funding programs with up to 100% profit splits and $200M+ in total payouts.
description_long:
## The Central Trade-Off: Cheap Entry, Narrow Instrument Menu

Funding Pips sits in the crowded prop firm category with a pitch that is easy to summarize: pay $29, prove you can hit a 6% target on a $5,000 simulated account, and keep up to 100% of the profits once funded. That entry price is genuinely low for the category, and the Trustpilot footprint (43,000+ reviews averaging 4.5/5) suggests a working payout pipeline. The catch is that Funding Pips is a forex-and-CFD shop. If you trade individual stocks, options, or US futures, this firm is the wrong door — the roughly 48 instruments on offer cover forex, indices, metals, energies, and crypto and nothing beyond that.

That single constraint decides whether the rest of the review matters to you. Everything below assumes you already know you want to trade currencies, indices, gold, oil, or crypto CFDs — because that is the entire universe Funding Pips supports.

## The Five Programs and Why They Exist

Funding Pips runs three evaluation styles stacked into five sample plans, and the pricing gaps between them tell you what the firm is actually charging for.

The **2-Step Pro $5K at $29** is the loss-leader — a stricter version of the standard 2-step with a lower 6% Phase 1 target, a tighter 3% daily loss limit, and an 80% profit split. It exists to get you in the door.

The **2-Step Standard $5K at $36** is what most traders should actually compare against competitors: 8% Phase 1 / 5% Phase 2 targets, no time limit, and profit splits scaling up to 100%. The $7 premium over the Pro plan buys you a looser daily loss rule and better upside.

The **2-Step Standard $50K at $289** is the same ruleset applied to a larger simulated balance. Ten times the account size at roughly eight times the fee — the classic prop firm curve where fee-to-account ratio improves as you scale up, provided you can actually pass the larger target in dollar terms.

The **1-Step $5K at $59** compresses evaluation into a single 10% profit target with no time limit, capped at a 90% split. You pay double the Standard 2-step fee to skip a phase; whether that is worth it depends entirely on how you handle the psychological difference between two 5-8% pushes and one 10% push on the same rules.

The **Zero (Instant Funding) $100K at $555** is the outlier. No evaluation, no profit target, 95% split, bi-weekly payouts, and a $7-per-lot commission on forex trades that will materially eat into scalping strategies. You are essentially renting a live-simulated account outright rather than earning one through a challenge.

## What the Rules Actually Constrain

Funding Pips advertises **no time limits** on the evaluation phases, which removes one of the more punishing pressures found at older prop firms. The daily loss limits — 3% on the Pro plan, higher on Standard — are the real behavioral cage. On a $5K account, a 3% daily limit is $150. That is a single mistimed news trade on gold or a wide EUR/USD stop after a Fed print. Traders coming from personal accounts routinely underestimate how quickly they can breach a daily drawdown when position sizing was tuned for a live retail account with no such rule.

The supplied data confirms **paper trading** (which is what the evaluation phase effectively is), **automated trading** support, and platform choice between MT5, cTrader, and Match Trader. The one platform tax worth flagging: **cTrader carries a $20 surcharge** on top of the challenge fee. If you already have muscle memory in cTrader, budget for it; otherwise MT5 is included at the base price.

What is not present, and matters for some traders: no built-in trade journaling, no news feed integrated into the platform layer, no proprietary mobile app. You get whatever the underlying platform (MT5, cTrader, Match Trader) provides on mobile, which is workable for position management but not a native Funding Pips experience.

## Payout Mechanics and Why Frequency Matters

Funding Pips advertises daily and weekly payout options on some plans, Tuesday / bi-weekly / on-demand on the Standard 2-step, and bi-weekly on the Zero program. In prop firm evaluation, payout frequency is not a nice-to-have — it is directly tied to how much of your account you can compound before the next withdrawal cycle. Faster payouts mean smaller reinvestment pool; slower payouts mean more capital working before you extract it.

The firm claims **$200M+ in total payouts** as of its current marketing, and the Trustpilot rating (4.5/5 from 43,000+ verified reviews) is consistent with a firm that is actually paying — a rating that high with that many reviews is difficult to fabricate. That said, Trustpilot is a lagging indicator. If Funding Pips changed its rules or payout structure last month, those reviews would not yet reflect it. I could not independently verify current payout processing times from the source material provided — treat the advertised cadences as targets, not guarantees, and cross-reference recent (last 30 days) reviews before committing.

## Who This Actually Fits

The honest buyer profile for Funding Pips is narrower than the marketing implies. It fits **forex-focused discretionary traders** who want a low-cost path to a larger simulated balance and are comfortable with CFD execution. It fits **intermediate traders testing new strategies** on someone else's capital rather than risking their own. It fits **algorithmic traders** who can run automated systems on MT5 or cTrader within the drawdown rules.

It does not fit **stock or options traders** — the instrument menu simply does not include them. It does not fit **US futures traders** — go to Topstep or Apex Trader Funding instead. It does not fit anyone who needs a **polished proprietary mobile app** for on-the-go management. And it does not fit traders who want **educational content or built-in journaling** as part of the package; Funding Pips is a funding mechanism, not a trading education product.

## Where Funding Pips Sits Against Alternatives

Against **FTMO**, Funding Pips is generally cheaper at entry and offers a wider range of program styles (2-step, 1-step, and instant), but FTMO has a longer operating history and more established reputation. Against **The5ers**, Funding Pips has a more conventional challenge structure — The5ers leans more into scaling plans and low-drawdown instant funding. Against **Topstep** and **Apex Trader Funding**, the comparison collapses because those firms are futures-focused and Funding Pips does not offer futures at all.

Company context worth knowing: Funding Pips is operated by **Ankh Prop – FZCO**, registered in the IFZA Business Park, Dubai. The firm launched in 2022, making it one of the newer entrants in a category where the older firms have weathered more industry turbulence (including the 2023-2024 MetaQuotes crackdown that forced several prop firms to change execution models). Dubai registration is common in this category but carries different regulatory implications than firms domiciled in the EU or US — worth understanding if regulatory recourse matters to you.

## The Decision in One Paragraph

Buy a Funding Pips challenge if you trade forex, metals, indices, energies, or crypto CFDs, want the lowest reasonable entry cost in the prop firm category, and accept that you are dealing with a firm founded in 2022 with a Dubai regulatory footprint. Skip Funding Pips if you need US futures, individual equities, options, a polished proprietary app, or the reassurance of a decade-plus operating history. The $29 entry point is legitimate — but a $29 challenge you fail because you did not respect a 3% daily loss limit is still $29 spent for nothing.

Multiple platform choices: MT5, cTrader, Match Trader
Trustpilot 4.5/5 from 43,000+ reviews suggests real payouts
cons: No individual stocks, options, or US futures — roughly 48 CFD instruments only
cTrader platform carries a $20 surcharge on top of the challenge fee
No proprietary mobile app — relies on third-party platform apps
Firm founded in 2022, shorter track record than FTMO or Topstep
3% daily loss limit on Pro plan is tight for volatile instruments
headquarters: IFZA Business Park, DDP, Dubai, UAE

Algorithmic traders running MT5 or cTrader automation
Cost-sensitive prop firm shoppers wanting the cheapest entry tier
Traders comfortable with Dubai-based regulatory footprint
